rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CALIFORNIA NURSES ASSOCIATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Enforce Arbitrator'S Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 8, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, Petition To Enforce Arbitrator's Subpoena Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. It would be helpful to the court if the parties were prepared to discuss their positions on the question of whether the IR is information within the sole purview of a protected committee under the holding of Willits v. Superior Court (Cal. App. 1993) 20 Cal. App. 4th 90, 104-105, and whether in-camera review of the document would be necessary for the court to determine that question. The Court denies Respondent's motion to strike Petitioner's reply brief, grants the motion for leave to file a surreply, and has considered both Petitioner's reply and Respondent's surreply. Pro-Tem Judge Halperin discloses that he is a co-chair of the Advisory Council to the 360 Center, an HIV clinical primary care practice at the UC Parnassus campus, and encourages the parties to raise any concerns they may have with respect to this disclosure. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: RHOMA YOUNG VS. ROBERT HENRY BLUMENTHAL, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order Compelling The Pltf To Serve Further Responses To Deft'S Interrogatories, Set #1 And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 8, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 3, Motion For Order Compelling The Plaintiff To Serve Further Responses To Defendant's Interrogatories, Set #1 And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Given the trial date of November 13, 2012, Plaintiff shall, no later than 5:00 P.M. on Monday, November 12th, provide verified responses to Interrogatories 1 through 30 identifying the specific documents reviewed and analyzed and the time spent on each. If Plaintiff does not have time information broken down by specific document, or does not have information concerning specific documents reviewed with respect to each line item of plaintiff's invoices, the interrogatory responses shall state that the plaintiff does not have such information. The request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY CURATOLO VS. JANE GRONA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 8, 2012, Line 21, DEFENDANT JAN CASEY'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court sustains the demurrer to the conversion cause of action. The statute of limitations for that claim is three years under CCP 338. Plaintiff relies on the discovery rule to avoid the statute of limitations. In order to invoke the discovery rule, the plaintiff must specifically plead facts which show (1) the time and manner of discovery and (2) the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence. See Saliter v. Pierce Brothers Mortuaries (1978) 81 Cal.App.3d 292, 297. Here, paragraph 20 alleges facts regarding the time and manner of discovery. Plaintiff, however, does not allege any facts regarding why the discovery could not have occurred earlier. Plaintiff has 20 days leave to amend to plead facts to support the second prong of the discovery rule. The Court rejects defendant's remaining arguments on the conversion cause of action. The demurrer to the unjust enrichment cause of action is overruled. The Court strikes the claim for punitive damages with leave to amend because plaintiff's tort claim against Jan Casey is possibly time-barred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY CURATOLO VS. JANE GRONA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 8, 2012, Line 21, DEFENDANT JANE GRONER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to breach of contract, breach of implied covenant and specific performance causes of action are overruled. Plaintiff properly pleads the existence and terms of the contract according to its legal intendment and effect. The Court sustains the demurrer to the conversion and fraud causes of action. The statute of limitations for those claims is three years under CCP 338. Plaintiff relies on the discovery rule to avoid the statute of limitations. In order to invoke the discovery rule, the plaintiff must specifically plead facts which show (1) the time and manner of discovery and (2) the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence. See Saliter v. Pierce Brothers Mortuaries (1978) 81 Cal.App.3d 292, 297. Here, paragraph 20 alleges facts regarding the time and manner of discovery. Plaintiff, however, does not allege any facts regarding why the discovery could not have occurred earlier. Plaintiff has 20 days leave to amend to plead facts to support the second prong of the discovery rule. The Court rejects defendant's remaining arguments on the fraud and conversion causes of action. The demurrer to the accounting cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff does not need to allege a fiduciary relationship to support that cause of action and the statute of limitations is 4 years. Estate of Peebles, (1972) 27 Cal.App.3d 163, 166. The demurrer to the unjust enrichment cause of action is overruled. The Court strikes the claim for punitive damages with leave to amend because plaintiff's tort claims are possibly time-barred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
